seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a RAG pipeline with Claude's native tool use enables powerful document retrieval, vector search, and synthesis with source attribution, leveraging Claude's agentic capabilities for dynamic querying. However, tool use chains in RAG systems are prone to cascading errors, hallucinations, and citation inaccuracies that undermine reliability. Vex guardrails wrap Claude's tool responses to verify source faithfulness, context relevance, and output consistency, ensuring production-grade reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The RAG pipeline begins with user query processing via Claude's tool use, where Claude invokes a vector search tool to retrieve relevant document chunks from a vector database like Pinecone or FAISS. Retrieved contexts are assembled and fed back to Claude, which uses additional tools for re-ranking and filtering before synthesizing a final response with inline citations. Claude's agentic loop handles iterative refinement, resolving ambiguities through parallel tool calls for metadata extraction and relevance scoring.

Tool failures early in the chain (e.g., malformed vector queries) cascade to poor synthesis, while parallel retrievals may yield conflicting contexts without resolution. Vex verification wraps each tool_use response to catch inconsistencies before they propagate. The final output includes source attribution, verified for accuracy against retrieved documents.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    ↓
Claude Agent (Tool Use)
    ↙️     ↘️
Vector Search  Metadata Tool
    ↓           ↓
Document Chunks  Context Filter
         ↓       ↓
     Context Assembly
         ↓
   Vex.verify() Guardrails
         ↓
Claude Synthesis + Citations
         ↓
Final Response

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early vector search tool errors produce empty or irrelevant contexts, leading to hallucinated synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs before context assembly, retrying failed calls

risks.name: Conflicting Retrievals
risks.description: Parallel tool calls return contradictory document chunks without resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-validates parallel results for consistency and relevance

risks.name: Hallucinated Citations
risks.description: Claude fabricates source references not present in retrieved contexts
risks.mitigation: Vex traces citations back to exact document chunks with similarity checks

risks.name: Context Drift
risks.description: Extended reasoning drifts from retrieved evidence, producing inconsistent outputs
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces source faithfulness across reasoning chain

implementation.title: Vector Search Tool Definition
implementation.description: Define Claude-compatible tool for Pinecone vector search with metadata filtering

implementation.title: Claude Agent Loop with Tool Use
implementation.description: Main agent loop handling tool calls and context assembly
implementation.code:
def run_rag_pipeline(query: str):
  messages = [{'role': 'user', 'content': query}]
  
  while True:
    response = client.messages.create(
      model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620',
      max_tokens=2000,
      tools=[vector_search_tool],
      messages=messages
    )
    
    if not response.stop_reason == 'tool_use':
      return response.content[0].text
    
    # Handle tool call
    tool_call = response.content[1]
    # Execute search and append results
    messages.append({'role': 'assistant', 'content': response.content[0]})
    messages.append(tool_call)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Tool Use with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Verify Claude's tool_use responses before processing to catch early failures
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import verify

response = client.messages.create(
  model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620',
  tools=[vector_search_tool],
  messages=messages
)

# Vex verification for tool calls
if response.stop_reason == 'tool_use':
  verified = verify(
    response,
    checks=[
      'tool_call_validity',
      'context_relevance',
      'no_hallucination'
    ],
    risk_profile='high'
  )
  
  if not verified:
    raise ValueError('Tool call failed verification')

# Execute verified tool call
